title: Indori Fort
tagline: Indori Fort — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Indori fort also known as Induri Fort (Marathi: इन्दुरी किल्ला)is one of the many Land forts of Maharashtra state in India.
overview.description: Indori fort also known as Induri Fort (Marathi: इन्दुरी किल्ला)is one of the many Land forts of Maharashtra state in India. Situated close to the hill station Lonavala and 35 km (22 mi) north of Pune, Indori fort rises to an elevation of 1,033 m (3,389 ft) above sea level. The fort is located on the banks of Indrayani River. The fort was under the Maratha Empire for the majority of the time.

topPlaces.name: Induri
topPlaces.description: Induri is a village and gram panchayat in India, situated in Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra. It encompasses an area of 1,531.07 ha (3,783 acres).

topPlaces.name: Nanoli Tarf Chakan
topPlaces.description: Nanoli Tarf Chakan is a village and gram panchayat in India, situated in Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra. It encompasses an area of 496 ha (1,226 acres).

topPlaces.name: Talegaon Dabhade
topPlaces.description: Talegaon is a town on the outskirts of the city of Pune, India.

topPlaces.name: Ambi, Mawal
topPlaces.description: Ambi is a village and gram panchayat in India, situated in Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra. It encompasses an area of 819 ha (2,024 acres).

topPlaces.name: Ghorawadi Caves
topPlaces.description: The Ghorawadi caves, also known as Ghoradeshwar caves or Shelarwadi caves, are around 25 km (16 mi) northwest of Pune, India, were originally Buddhist caves, and now contain carvings and statues of Buddhist and Hindu deities. They are close to the NH4 highway connecting Pune and Mumbai.

topPlaces.name: Shirgaon (Mawal)
topPlaces.description: Shirgaon is a village and gram panchayat in Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra, India. It encompasses an area of 389.66 ha (963 acres).

topPlaces.name: Somatane
topPlaces.description: Somatane is an Indian village and gram panchayat located in Mawal taluka of Pune district, Maharashtra. It encompasses an area of 434.04 ha (1,073 acres).

topPlaces.name: B K Birla Centre For Education
topPlaces.description: B K Birla Centre For Education is a Boys' residential school located near Pune, India. Affiliated to the Central Board of Secondary Education syllabus, it caters for 600 students from class IV to XII. The school's first principal was G. C. Rao, who was further succeeded by S. K. Bhardwaj and then Subhash Kumar.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Indori Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Indori Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Indori Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pune Airport (28 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (29 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Indori Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Indori Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Indori Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Indori Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Indori Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Induri, Nanoli Tarf Chakan, Talegaon Dabhade, Ambi, Mawal, Ghorawadi Caves.
